channel.title: CS-207 Graphs & Network Algorithms :: CS Two Year Refactor
channel.description: CS-207 — Graphs & Network Algorithms 1 credit Working description Graph traversal, shortest path, minimum spanning trees — named explicitly as optimization (best path/structure under a metric). Graphs as the most general representation (terminal Data Structures pass). Geospatial algorithms: routing/shortest-path on road networks (weighted graphs; Dijkstra, A* as geospatially-motivated heuristic search); spatial joins and range/nearest-neighbor queries using B4 spatial indexes. Signature assessment: Design Review begins here. Computational Models pass: a brief see-and-explore example of a LOGIC/CONSTRAINT model (a few Prolog-style facts/rules + a query, or a small constraint problem a solver resolves) — connects to MATH-101 predicate logic, SQL’s logical character, and the Optimization lens.
